meta.name: Cluster & neighborhood enrichment (raw → spatial)
meta.description: End-to-end on unprocessed spatial data (e.g. Xenium): cluster the cells, build the spatial graph, then test which clusters are spatially co-enriched.
meta.provenance: Combines the scanpy preprocessing-to-clustering path with the squidpy neighborhood-enrichment vignette; targets unprocessed raw-count spatial data such as Xenium.

readme: End-to-end on unprocessed spatial data: cluster the cells (scanpy), build a generic (non-grid) spatial graph, then run the neighborhood-enrichment permutation test grouped by the freshly computed clusters. `cluster_key` names the obs column the clustering writes and the enrichment then groups by.

params.name: cluster_key
params.tooltip: obs column the Leiden clustering writes and the enrichment groups by

params.name: resolution
params.tooltip: Leiden resolution: higher = more, smaller clusters

params.name: coord_type
params.tooltip: spatial-graph geometry: generic for cell-resolution data, grid for Visium hexagons

params.name: n_neighs
params.tooltip: neighbors per node in the spatial graph
